figures.caption: Table 29: Family-wise candidate screening and the resulting BestSingleDerived control. The selected single-derived control is reported explicitly so that the main benchmark compares DerivOpt against the strongest transparent single-derived baseline in each family.

Table 2 aggregates over all family, backbone, budget, and retained-scale configurations. The canonical ladder becomes benchmark-wide: DerivOpt is best in every aggregate column, attaining the lowest mean rollout nRMSE (0.074), the lowest mean final fineRel (0.748), the highest mean T gen
Table 5: Canonical NS selector-adequacy diagnostics. Lower is better for the anisotropy and residualcorrelation diagnostics; higher is better for selection agreement and rollout-agreement.

Table 14: Aggregate input-stage mechanism broken down by PDE family. Each cell reports mean fineRel(0) and mean PassRatein, in that order for that family; lower is better for the first quantity and higher is better for the second. Family win-count records the number of families in which the state attains the strongest overall mechanism profile.
